title: rhamnus frangula var. pumila
intro: Rhamnus frangula var. pumila, also known as dwarf alder buckthorn, is a small shrub or tree species that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anthraquinone glycosides that stimulate bowel movements and have laxative effects. The health benefits of Rhamnus frangula var. pumila include its ability to treat constipation, jaundice, and liver disorders, as well as its use as a diuretic and a treatment for gallstones. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and promote detoxification.
